question.text: Under Section 23C of SCRA, what penalty applies to a stockbroker who fails to redress the grievances of an investor after being directed by SEBI or Stock Exchange?

answer.text: Rs. 50,000 minimum, up to Rs. 50 lakh maximum
Rs. 5 lakh minimum, up to Rs. 25 crore maximum
Rs. 1 lakh minimum, up to Rs. 1 crore maximum
Rs. 10 lakh minimum, up to Rs. 10 crore maximum

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 23C provides that a stockbroker failing to redress investor grievances shall be liable to a penalty not less than Rs. 1 lakh, extendable to Rs. 1 lakh per day, subject to a maximum of Rs. 1 crore. **The Trap:** Rs. 5 lakh minimum is the penalty under Section 23E for listing condition breaches, not investor grievance redressal. **Cross-Exam Context:** Investor grievance redressal mechanisms are covered across multiple NISM certifications. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Timely redressal of investor grievances is not just a regulatory requirement but also good business practice.
